description:
You are working as an observability engineer.  
    A colleague just left you a copy of the node’s resolver
    configuration in the file  

        /home/user/observability/node.resolv

    The file is already present on the system and its exact
    contents are:

        # Generated by NetworkManager
        search corp.local
        nameserver 10.98.0.2
        nameserver 10.98.0.3
        options timeout:1 attempts:3

    Your immediate goal is to verify which DNS server the node
    will try first.  
    Using a **single** terminal command, extract **only** the first
    IPv4 address that appears on a line beginning with the keyword
    “nameserver” in `/home/user/observability/node.resolv` and write
    that exact address to a new file

        /home/user/observability/primary_dns.log

    Output format that the automated grader will check:
    • The file must contain exactly one line.  
    • That line must be the IPv4 address itself (no leading or
      trailing spaces, no comments, no additional text).  
    • The line must end with a single newline character `\n`.

    Do not rename or move the provided `node.resolv`.  
    Do not create any extra files or directories.
truth:
<!-- Verification data for the automated test -->
    <initial_files>
        <file path="/home/user/observability/node.resolv"><![CDATA[# Generated by NetworkManager
search corp.local
nameserver 10.98.0.2
nameserver 10.98.0.3
options timeout:1 attempts:3
]]></file>
    </initial_files>

    <expected_output>
        <file path="/home/user/observability/primary_dns.log"><![CDATA[10.98.0.2
]]></file>
    </expected_output>
name: task_000000_0228cd64
